title: Stack the sheets
intro: Your cell needs 150 µA of drive but the footprint is frozen — you cannot make the terrain wider on the wafer. FinFETs add parallel hills side by side; nanosheets stack whole channels VERTICALLY, like floors in a parking garage, each floor its own ball crowd with its own gated hill. Build enough effective width into the same footprint.
explain: This is GAA's killer feature: effective width = sheets × perimeter, so drive current scales vertically while the footprint stands still. Real 3 nm/2 nm-class devices stack 3–4 sheets, and because the gate wraps every sheet on all four sides, leakage stays fenced while you stack — your FinFET at max fins delivered less current AND more leakage than the 4-sheet stack. Bonus knob: sheet width is a continuous design variable fins never had.
